rules: NEVER include export calls in blender_script — blender_tool.py injects the export automatically based on export_format param.
Always clear the default scene at the start: bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT') + bpy.ops.object.delete()
To remove a specific named object use bpy.data.objects.remove(bpy.data.objects['Name'], do_unlink=True) — more reliable headless than select+delete.
Always name objects explicitly: obj.name = 'SomeName' — unnamed objects are hard to reference later.
Apply modifiers before exporting to GLB/STL or the mesh may not reflect them.
Smooth shading: bpy.ops.object.shade_smooth() — call AFTER selecting the object.
Track To constraint requires track_axis='TRACK_NEGATIVE_Z' and up_axis='UP_Y' for standard camera pointing.
Scale a cube by setting obj.scale then calling bpy.ops.object.transform_apply(scale=True) for true mesh dimensions.
bpy.context.active_object is the object just created by the last primitive_*_add call.
Lights: use type='AREA' for soft, type='SPOT' for directional, type='SUN' for outdoor scenes.
print('HAYEONG_OK: ...') at the end of the script — used to confirm execution in logs.

pattern: obj.data.materials.append(mat)  # or obj.data.materials[0] = mat if slot exists

common_errors.no_active_object: Call select_all + delete before adding primitives, or set bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = obj explicitly.
common_errors.context_override: In headless mode bpy.ops.* may fail with 'context is incorrect'. Prefer bpy.data.* and bpy.context.* for non-operator operations.
common_errors.export_already_in_script: NEVER add export calls — blender_tool injects them. Double-export causes errors.
common_errors.scale_not_applied: GLB/STL may look wrong if scale is not applied. Always transform_apply(scale=True) after scaling.
common_errors.object_not_found: bpy.data.objects['Name'] raises KeyError if the name doesn't exist. Check obj.name assignments.
common_errors.shade_smooth_no_selection: bpy.ops.object.shade_smooth() requires the object to be active AND selected.
common_errors.duplicate_material_name: bpy.data.materials.new() does NOT overwrite existing materials — it creates 'Mat.001' etc. Use unique names per run.

print_scale.note: 1 Blender unit = 1 meter. For 3D printing, always think in meters. 50mm = 0.05, 100mm = 0.1, 200mm = 0.2. A typical small figurine (100mm tall) = 0.1 units height. When James specifies a size in mm, convert to meters before setting dimensions.
